engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is trusting a healthcare institution and its practitioners — the site sells defined medical expertise via accreditation/halal certs, director's note, and named specialist-doctor profiles, which lands on 'trust' at the top of the ladder.

goal.one_action: Register online for care (via the hospital's registration app) or reach a service line by phone/WhatsApp

structure.archetype_note: Sprawling institutional hospital portal (40 visible pages): service detail pages, doctor directory, separate schedule/registration engines, blog. Not a single-conversion landing page.

mapped.observed_as: service highlight blocks (cardiac cath, MCU, home care)
Layanan Operasi / Women care SURGERY service detail

unmapped.observed: Multiple purpose-specific WhatsApp service lines (Customer Care, NersQ post-admission, Tele Farmasi medication ordering)
unmapped.why_no_fit: Not a single CTA — a directory of role-segmented contact channels, closer to a service switchboard than any canonical section

unmapped.observed: DOKTERKU doctor directory + separate JADWAL DOKTER schedule engine
unmapped.why_no_fit: A browsable practitioner directory + external scheduling portal, not a proof section or a single team grid

proposed_new.name: practitioner-profiles
proposed_new.evidence: DOKTERKU directory of general/specialist/sub-specialist/dental doctors plus per-specialty doctor lists across service pages — per-person profiles as a browsable property, matching the registry's practitioner-profiles slug.

confidence.why: Capture is full, but the site is a 40-page institution with a fragmented set of conversion channels (app registration, multiple WhatsApp lines, external schedule engine) and the schedule/registration pages weren't crawled, so the single 'primary action' is genuinely ambiguous.

gap_findings: Site's job is not one conversion action: it spans an off-site app registration flow, a separate doctor-schedule engine, and multiple role-segmented WhatsApp lines — outside the single-conversion ICP.
Role-segmented contact switchboard (Customer Care / post-admission / tele-pharmacy WhatsApp lines) has no canonical section — cta collapses distinct service intents into one.
packages capability lists MCU tiers but the site deliberately shows NO prices — a common trust-sector pattern the packages/pricing sections assume prices for.
